mnemonics: Every Turing Machine Can Decide Regular Languages (ETMCDRL) - Hierarchy of languages: Regular (DFA) < Context-Free (PDA) < Decidable (TM) < Turing-Recognizable (TM)
NP: No Problem? (Actually, Non-deterministic Polynomial time, but the mnemonic highlights the 'big question' of P vs NP)
Pumping Lemma: PLR - Pump for Length > p, to show Language is not Regular
keyPoints: A language is regular if it can be recognized by a Finite Automaton (DFA/NFA).
A Turing Machine is the most powerful computational model; it can simulate any algorithm.
The Church-Turing Thesis states that any effectively computable function is computable by a Turing Machine.
P vs NP is the question of whether problems verifiable in polynomial time are also solvable in polynomial time.
commonMistakes: Confusing Turing-Decidable (halts for all inputs) with Turing-Recognizable (may loop on some inputs).
Misapplying the Pumping Lemma by trying to prove a language is regular instead of proving it is not regular.
Assuming Non-Deterministic Finite Automata (NFA) are more powerful than Deterministic Finite Automata (DFA); they are not (they have equal power).
